title: Build real document workflows
description: Start from runnable source, follow a focused guide, and inspect the APIs behind each generated artifact.

cards.title: Validated Word document proof pack
cards.description: Generate template-driven documents, HTML and Markdown conversions, and a structured comparison with diagnostics and Open XML validation.
cards.proof: The example writes source inputs, DOCX outputs, round-trip content where applicable, validation results, and a machine-readable manifest.

cards.title: Operational Excel report
cards.description: Turn structured application data into a workbook with worksheets, tables, formatting, formulas, and charts.
cards.proof: The DomainDetective example is runnable source that produces a real XLSX report rather than a static mockup.

cards.title: End-to-end presentation proof
cards.description: Build a presentation with slides, text, images, tables, charts, notes, and package validation from one executable example.
cards.proof: The proof example saves PPTX output and checks the generated package so the workflow can be evaluated from source to artifact.

cards.title: Mailbox conversion with verification
cards.description: Read PST, OST, OLM, EMLX, Maildir, Apple Mail, and mailbox directories, then export selected or complete store content.
cards.proof: PST export reports converted and skipped items and can verify message semantics without persisting private message values.

cards.title: Data-driven architecture diagram
cards.description: Generate VSDX architecture, network, process, sequence, timeline, and graph diagrams from application data.
cards.proof: Runnable examples validate the VSDX package and can export SVG or PNG previews without Microsoft Visio installed.

cards.title: Multi-format extraction pipeline
cards.description: Detect and extract normalized text, tables, visuals, assets, and chunks across Office documents, email stores, PDF, Markdown, and more.
cards.proof: The committed Reader baseline exercises 25 cases across 14 formats and records the runtime, machine context, and validation lane.
